refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly state the latency metric and its impact on safety-critical teleoperation, focusing on measurable performance.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data and validate p95 latency ≤ 150 ms during teleoperation scenarios.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Ensure real-time processing and forwarding of teleoperation commands and telemetry within the NGHE solution.
FR3: Provide latency monitoring and alerting to detect and handle latency violations.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and QoS prioritization for teleoperation data.
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes close to the vehicle to reduce processing delays.
DP3: Design telemetry and tasking data pipelines with minimal buffering and efficient serialization.
DP4: Integrate latency monitoring modules with real-time alerting.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and QoS prioritization for teleoperation data.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between network protocol optimization and telemetry data handling.
Dependency on network infrastructure and vehicle communication modules.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically addresses the latency requirement with minimal cross-domain coupling; other DPs can be modularized.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network infrastructure supports QoS and protocol optimizations.
Risk of network variability impacting latency despite protocol optimizations.
Assumes vehicle and teleoperation modules can interface with optimized protocols.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 (edge computing) if network conditions degrade or latency targets are not met.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Realization of teleoperation communication including protocol handling, telemetry processing, and latency management.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Telemetry data flows bidirectionally between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Teleoperation receives tasking commands from NGHE Solution module.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network interface for communication with remote operator.

module_containment_scores.notes: High-level coordination but not focused on latency-critical teleoperation logic.
Contains vehicle-side telemetry endpoints but not responsible for protocol optimization.
Primary module for teleoperation latency management and communication protocol handling.
Focuses on safety alerts, not on communication latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP1 to Teleoperation module centralizes latency-critical communication logic, minimizing diffusion and improving ownership clarity.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle module telemetry endpoints.
Dependency on network infrastructure outside module control.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor interface complexity and consider interface standardization to reduce coupling.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP1 directly addresses the refined FR with clear latency optimization focus and measurable verification.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists if telemetry processing is duplicated outside Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment score and low diffusion, supporting clear ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified end-to-end using telemetry and network monitoring tools within assigned IM boundaries.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency-critical functions to Teleoperation module with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Refine interface definitions between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules to reduce coupling.
Consider adding latency monitoring sub-FR and corresponding DP/IM in next decomposition level.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition and assignments are sound, adding explicit latency monitoring and alerting FR and corresponding DP/IM will improve completeness and safety assurance.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Ensure real-time processing and forwarding of teleoperation commands and telemetry within the NGHE solution.
FR3: Provide latency monitoring and alerting to detect and handle latency violations.

revise.why: To explicitly include latency monitoring and alerting as a sub-FR to address safety criticality and operational awareness.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports QoS and protocol optimizations.
Vehicle and Teleoperation modules can interface effectively over known interfaces.
open_risks: Network variability may still cause latency spikes beyond control.
Interface complexity between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules may increase coupling.
evidence_needed: Latency measurement data under operational network conditions.
Verification of protocol optimization effectiveness in Teleoperation module.
